questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.solution: E

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some engineers are doctors. No doctor is lawyer. All lawyers are rich. Conclusions: I. Some engineers are not lawyers. II. Some rich are not doctors.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's energy demand is expected to grow at ____ % annually.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The car (A) / which he (B) / is driving (C) / is a Mercedes. (D)
questions.solution: No error

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Which of the following was a social cost of the Industrial Revolution?
options: Poor working conditions and child labour
questions.solution: Poor working conditions and child labour

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / better than (B) / any student (C) / in the class. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'any other student'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

The Industrial Revolution began in which country?
questions.solution: Britain

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The commitee (A) / has given (B) / their report (C) / on time. (D)
questions.solution: A - should be 'committee'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

What powered steam engines during the Industrial Revolution?
questions.solution: Coal

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Neither the manager (A) / nor his assistants (B) / was present (C) / at the meeting. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'were present'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

The PLI scheme is aimed at:
questions.solution: Reducing dependence on solar imports

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The company (A) / is in (B) / the red (C) / for three years. (D)
options: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'
questions.solution: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

The word 'abundant' in the passage most nearly means:
questions.solution: Plentiful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: It is (A) / high time (B) / we leave (C) / for the station. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'left'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

The coal found in Gondwana fields is primarily:
questions.solution: Bituminous

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: She not only (A) / lost her wallet (B) / but also (C) / her mobile phone. (D)
options: A - should be 'She lost not only'
questions.solution: A - should be 'She lost not only'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

Which of the following is mentioned as a concern related to coal usage?
questions.solution: Environmental degradation and climate change

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He asked (A) / that whether (B) / I could (C) / help him. (D)
questions.solution: B - remove 'that' or 'whether'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a risk in underground mining?
questions.solution: Earthquake

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The book is superior ____ the other one.
questions.solution: to

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The company ____ a new subsidiary last year.
questions.solution: established

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of EXUBERANT:
questions.solution: Energetic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To go the extra mile':
questions.solution: To make extra effort

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of CONUNDRUM:
questions.solution: Solution

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To see eye to eye':
questions.solution: To agree with someone

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PERILOUS:
questions.solution: Safe

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ABSTAIN:
questions.solution: Refrain

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Barchan dune:
options: Crescent-shaped, horns downwind. In areas of limited sand supply
questions.solution: Barchan: single direction wind. Moves by sand avalanching on slip face.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Liquid immiscibility in magmas:
options: Fe-rich (ferrobasalt) + Si-rich (rhyolite). Immiscible globules. Skaergaard, Palisades
questions.solution: Liquid immiscibility: Fe-Ti-P enrichment. Carbonatite-silicate immiscibility also.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 30% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+30+20) = 42%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seafloor spreading rate: full rate =
options: Rate of separation of two plates at ridge
questions.solution: Full rate: total separation velocity. Half rate: half. Slow: <5cm/yr. Fast: >8cm/yr. Ultrafast: >15cm/yr.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Hotspot track indicates:
options: Plate motion over stationary mantle plume
questions.solution: Hotspot: deep mantle plume. Age-progressive volcanic chain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Foraminifera biostratigraphy:
options: Planktonic (globigerinids) for Mesozoic-Cenozoic. Benthic for paleobathymetry. δ¹⁸O in tests
questions.solution: Forams: CaCO₃ tests. Planktonic: index fossils for Cretaceous-Tertiary. Cretaceous-Paleogene boundary.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Oxygenation event ~2.4 Ga (GOE) caused by:
questions.solution: GOE: Great Oxidation Event. Cyanobacteria produced O2. Banded iron formations ended. Mass extinction of anaerobes.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Rare earth element deposits often associated with:
questions.solution: REE: concentrated in alkaline complexes, carbonatites.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Fission track dating uses:
questions.solution: Fission tracks: 238U spontaneous fission creates damage. Etched and counted. Annealing temperature determines closure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granulite:
options: High-grade metamorphic. No hydrous minerals (pyroxene + feldspar + garnet)
questions.solution: Granulite: dry metamorphism. H2O absent. Pyroxene stable, biotite unstable.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Reaction rim (coronite) texture indicates:
questions.solution: Coronite: rim of new minerals between reacting minerals. Indicates P-T conditions changed, minerals reacting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quaternary in India:
questions.solution: Karewa: Pleistocene lake deposits. Loess in Kashmir. Sea level changes.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Karst topography develops in:
questions.solution: Karst: dissolution of soluble rock. Features: sinkholes, caves, disappearing streams, dolines, poljes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Foundation on rock:
options: Spread footing, raft, pile (socketed into rock). Rock socket friction + end bearing
questions.solution: Socketed piles: shear resistance along rock socket. Roughness increases capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pressure solution cleavage:
options: Strain accommodation by dissolution (Stylolite, dissolution seams). Insoluble residue (clay, organics, pyrite)
questions.solution: Pressure solution: σ₁ perpendicular / σ₃ parallel. Pressure shadow: fibers in low-stress zones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Arroyo:
options: Steep-walled, flat-floored ephemeral channel in arid regions. Incised
questions.solution: Arroyo: incision by base level fall or climate change. SW USA. Valley fill.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magma mixing:
options: Mixing of two magma batches. Disequilibrium textures: sieve (plagioclase), reverse zoning (augite overgrowth)
questions.solution: Mixing: banded pumice, heterogeneous phenocrysts. Mixing + fractional crystallization (MFC).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 20 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.05. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Point groups and symmetry:
options: 32 crystallographic point groups. m (mirror), 2, 3, 4, 6 (rotation axes), 1 (inversion), 2₁ (screw), etc.
questions.solution: Hermann-Mauguin: n (rotation), n̄ (rotoinversion), n/m (mirror ⊥ axis), mm (mirror ∥ axis).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pressure shadows and fibers:
options: Fibers grow in extension direction (σ₃) in low-pressure zones around rigid porphyroclasts
questions.solution: Pressure shadows: fibers of quartz, calcite grow in extension direction. Syntaxial vs antitaxial growth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic discontinuities: Conrad discontinuity:
questions.solution: Conrad: mid-crustal. Vp jump. Not everywhere. Some continents: no Conrad.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Plagioclase feldspar series ranges from:
questions.solution: Plagioclase: NaAlSi3O8 to CaAl2Si2O8.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rb-Sr dating:
options: ⁸⁷Rb → ⁸⁷Sr (β⁻). T½ = 48.8 Ga. Whole-rock isochron. Initial ⁸⁷Sr/⁸⁶Sr ratio
questions.solution: Rb-Sr: isochron method. Initial ratio for magma source (mantle 0.703, crust 0.710).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Basalt as construction material:
options: Strong, dense. Good aggregate. Columnar joints weaken rock mass
questions.solution: Basalt: high UCS (200-350 MPa). Columnar joints → blocky excavation. Used for roads, concrete.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A reverse (or thrust) fault is produced mainly by:
questions.solution: Compression pushes the hanging wall up over the footwall — a reverse/thrust fault.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geoid: equipotential surface of gravity:
options: Varies ±100m from ellipsoid. Anomalies from density variations in mantle
questions.solution: Geoid: sea level if ocean at rest. Positive geoid = dense material below. Negative = less dense.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Electrical resistivity of rocks decreases with:
questions.solution: Resistivity: water-saturated rocks conduct better. Archie equation: R = Rw/φ^m × Sw^-n. More water = lower R.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rhyolite:
questions.solution: Rhyolite: volcanic felsic. May contain glass (obsidian) or vesicles (pumice).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plagioclase — anorthite content:
options: An content: An0-10 albite, An10-30 oligoclase, An30-50 andesine, An50-70 labradorite, An70-90 bytownite, An90-100 anorthite
questions.solution: Continuous solid solution. NaAlSi3O8 (albite) to CaAl2Si2O8 (anorthite).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Basalt is a(n):
questions.solution: Basalt is the fine-grained, dark, mafic rock formed from rapidly cooled lava.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Foraminifera: wall types:
questions.solution: Forams: test types. Planktonic: globigerina (Cretaceous-Recent). Benthic: benthic forams.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetotelluric (MT) method:
options: Natural EM field (lightning, solar wind). Measures impedance Z = E/H
questions.solution: MT: deep crustal imaging. Low frequency → deep. Resistivity model. MTZ: mid-crustal conductor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chloritoid:
options: Fe-Mn-Al silicate. Otter-shaped twins. Low-grade metamorphic
questions.solution: Chloritoid: in low-medium grade metapelites. Ottrelite: Mn-rich variety.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Deep sea facies — turbidite:
options: Bouma sequence: Ta (massive), Tb (laminated), Tc (ripple), Td (parallel), Te (pelagic)
questions.solution: Bouma: graded bed (Ta) → pelagic (Te). Submarine fan. Thick-bedded near channel axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carbonate classification (Dunham):
options: Mudstone (<10% grains), Wackestone (>10% grains mud-supported), Packstone (grain-supported muddy), Grainstone (grain-supported mud-free)
questions.solution: Dunham: based on depositional texture. Binds tone, framestone, rudstone, floatstone added.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fluvial depositional systems:
options: Braided (coarse, high gradient) and meandering (fine, low gradient)
questions.solution: Braided: gravel bars. Meandering: point bars, floodplains. Both in alluvial settings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delta facies:
questions.solution: Gilbert delta: coarse-grained. River-dominated (birdfoot, Mississippi). Tide/wave-modified deltas.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Submarine fan channels vs lobes:
options: Channels: erosional, confined. Lobes: depositional, unconfined, compensational stacking
questions.solution: Channels: bypass conduits. Lobes: depocenters. Compensation stacking: lobes fill topographic lows.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chlorite group:
options: Green, platy, low-grade metamorphic mineral. Mg-Fe-Al sheet silicate
questions.solution: Chlorite: common in greenschist facies. Prograde: chlorite → biotite at higher grade.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic refraction first arrival interpretation: crossover distance:
options: Distance where refracted wave overtakes direct wave arrival
questions.solution: Crossover: x_c = 2h√((V₂+V₁)/(V₂-V₁)). h = thickness of first layer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ore reserve classification: measured, indicated, inferred:
options: Measured: highest confidence. Indicated: reasonable confidence. Inferred: lowest confidence
questions.solution: JORC, NI 43-101 codes. Measured: close spacing. Indicated: wider spacing. Inferred: limited data.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 2 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 4.5 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 2×4.5 = 9.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Structure contour map:
options: Lines of equal elevation on a geological surface (bedding, fault)
questions.solution: Structure contours: V-shaped across valleys (opposite rule for dipping beds).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+20+10) = 66%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater flow net:
options: Flow lines orthogonal to equipotential lines. Q = KH·(N_f/N_d). Used for seepage
questions.solution: Flow net: squares (curvilinear). N_f = flow channels. N_d = head drops. For dams, excavations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of kyanite:
questions.solution: Kyanite (Al2SiO5) — BLUE, hardness 4.5-7 depending on direction. Highly anisotropic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Wave-cut platform width controlled by:
options: Rate of sea-level change, rock resistance, wave energy
questions.solution: Platform: wider in weak rock, high wave energy, stable sea level. Narrow: resistant rock, rapid sea-level change.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: InSAR (Interferometric SAR):
options: Phase difference between two SAR images → ground deformation (mm precision)
questions.solution: InSAR: surface displacement. Subsidence, earthquakes, volcanoes. PS-InSAR for time series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Liquefaction:
options: Saturated loose sand → loses strength during shaking. Water table rise
questions.solution: Liquefaction: Sand boils, lateral spreading, settlement. SPT-based triggering assessment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Graphic texture:
options: Quartz rods in feldspar — intergrowth. Like cuneiform writing
questions.solution: Graphic: quartz-feldspar intergrowth. Simultaneous crystallization of eutectic-like melt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Permian-Triassic boundary in India:
options: In Gondwana: top of Raniganj Formation. Panchet Formation overlies
questions.solution: P-T boundary: extinction event. In Kashmir: Guryul Ravine (marine section).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Evaporite sequence:
options: Carbonate → gypsum → halite → K-Mg salts (increasing solubility)
questions.solution: Evaporites: restricted basin, high evaporation. Zechstein, Mediterranean Messinian.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: P-T path (counterclockwise):
options: Heating→burial→exhumation. Contact metamorphism overprinted by regional
questions.solution: CCW: high T first, then P increase. Plume-related metamorphism. Cordierite+andalusite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude of an earthquake is commonly expressed on the:
questions.solution: Earthquake size is reported as magnitude (Richter / moment magnitude).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ore reserve classification (JORC): indicated resource requires:
options: Sufficient exploration for geological confidence but lower than measured
questions.solution: Indicated: enough data for planning. Inferred: limited data. Measured: high confidence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: After 3 half-lives, the percentage of the original radioactive parent isotope remaining is:
questions.solution: Fraction remaining = (1/2)^3 = 12.5%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 60° shows a width of 10 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 10×sin60° ≈ 8.66 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4 m seam over 4 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.85. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 4M m²×4×1.4×0.85 ≈ 19 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Saltwater intrusion:
options: Freshwater-saltwater interface. Ghyben-Herzberg: z = 40·h (h = head above sea level)
questions.solution: Ghyben-Herzberg: z = ρ_f·h/(ρ_s-ρ_f). For ρ_s/ρ_f = 1.025: z ≈ 40h.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Twinning in plagioclase:
questions.solution: Polysynthetic twinning diagnostic of plagioclase under microscope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delta types:
questions.solution: Delta: river sediment deposition at coast. Tidal/wave influence shapes delta type.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LiDAR:
options: Laser scanning. High-resolution DEM. Penetrates vegetation canopy
questions.solution: LiDAR: 3D point cloud. Bare earth DEM. Used for fault mapping, forestry, flood modeling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Xenolith:
options: Foreign rock fragment in igneous rock. Enclave
questions.solution: Xenolith: wall rock fragment. Used to study mantle composition and crustal contamination.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transgression vs regression:
options: Transgression: sea advances (fining up). Regression: sea retreats (coarsening up)
questions.solution: Transgressive: sandstone → shale → limestone. Regressive: limestone → shale → sandstone.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Geophysical well logging measures:
options: Physical properties of formations surrounding borehole
questions.solution: Well logging: lowered tools measure resistivity, density, sonic, gamma ray, neutron. Identifies lithology, fluids.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Strike and dip:
options: Strike: trend of horizontal line on inclined plane. Dip: angle to horizontal
questions.solution: Strike: compass direction. Dip: angle + direction (e.g., 30° SE).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 54 g has a volume of 20 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 54/20 = 2.7.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rockfall protection:
options: Barrier, catch ditch, mesh, rock bolt, shotcrete. Warning systems
questions.solution: Rockfall: netting (draped, anchored). Attenuator barriers. Risk: exposure × probability × consequence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diagenesis:
options: Physical/chemical changes after deposition, before metamorphism (compaction, cementation, dissolution)
questions.solution: Diagenesis: near-surface, low T (<200°C). Cementation: quartz overgrowths, calcite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater quality parameters:
options: TDS, pH, hardness, Cl, NO₃, Fe, F, As. WHO/BIS standards
questions.solution: Fluoride: 0.5-1.5 mg/L. Arsenic: <0.01 mg/L. Nitrate: <45 mg/L. TDS: <500 mg/L.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magmatic ore deposits: chromitite layers in layered intrusions formed by:
options: Settling of chromite crystals from basaltic magma
questions.solution: Layered intrusions: Bushveld, Stillwater. Chromitite: cumulate layers. Chromite settles from magma.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock sample of 100 cm³ contains 15 cm³ of pore space. Its porosity is:
questions.solution: Porosity = (void volume/total volume)×100 = 15/100 = 15%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Miarolitic cavity:
options: Small vug in granite with inward-facing crystals. Late-stage volatiles
questions.solution: Miarolitic: miarolitic texture. Crystal-lined cavities. Indicates shallow emplacement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Karst topography:
options: Dissolution of limestone → sinkholes, caves, underground streams, tower karst
questions.solution: Karst: CaCO₃ dissolved by carbonic acid. Cockpit karst, cone karst, tower karst.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stalactite vs stalagmite:
options: Stalactite: hangs from ceiling. Stalagmite: grows from floor
questions.solution: Stalactite: CaCO₃ precipitation. Stalagmite: below dripline. Column: when they meet.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: InSAR:
options: Interferometric SAR. Measures ground deformation (mm-scale). Earthquake, volcano, subsidence
questions.solution: InSAR: two radar images of same area. Phase difference = displacement. Sentinel-1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plagioclase twin laws:
questions.solution: Plagioclase: polysynthetic twinning diagnostic in thin section. Lamellae under crossed polars.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Differential Thermal Analysis (DTA) detects:
questions.solution: DTA: measures temperature difference between sample and reference. Exothermic/endothermic peaks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 50 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.05. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 1 km², is 2 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 1×10⁶ m²×2 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 2.6 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Finite strain ellipsoid: X≥Y≥Z. Stretch S = L/L₀. For simple shear γ=1:
questions.solution: Simple shear γ=1: X/Z = γ+√(γ²+1) = 1+√2 ≈ 2.41. Strain ratio R=X/Y.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Streak of limonite:
questions.solution: Limonite (FeO(OH)·nH2O) — yellow-brown streak. Hydrous iron oxide.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Heave in excavation:
options: Upward water pressure exceeds effective stress. Quick condition
questions.solution: Heave: in sand near excavation. Critical gradient ~1. Sheet pile depth for safety.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the faulted block shown, the hanging wall has moved up over the footwall. This is a:
questions.solution: Hanging wall up = reverse fault, caused by compression.

figure.caption: reverse fault

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: C-14 dating:
options: ¹⁴C (T½ = 5730 yr). For organic material <50 ka. Atmospheric production, calibration curve
questions.solution: C-14: radiocarbon. β⁻ decay. Libby (1949). Calibration: tree rings, corals, speleothems.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pyroclastic rocks:
options: Formed from volcanic ejecta (ash, lapilli, bombs, blocks)
questions.solution: Tuff (ash), lapillistone, agglomerate (bombs). Welded tuff = ignimbrite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+35+15) = 42%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Blueschist facies: key mineral glaucophane:
options: Blue Na-amphibole. Stable at high P, low T (subduction)
questions.solution: Glaucophane: Na₂(Mg₃Al₂)Si₈O₂₂(OH)₂. Blue color. Subduction zone metamorphism.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stock:
questions.solution: Stock: similar to batholith but smaller. Often associated with mineral deposits.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Banded iron formation represents:
options: Precipitation of iron and silica from seawater
questions.solution: BIF: alternating iron-rich and silica-rich layers. ~2.5-1.8 Ga.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 8% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+20+8) = 68%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gneissic banding:
options: Alternating light (felsic) and dark (mafic) mineral layers
questions.solution: Gneiss: high grade. Segregation into bands. Compositional layering.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Earth's outer core is inferred to be liquid because:
options: Both P- and S-waves speed up there
questions.solution: Shear (S) waves cannot travel through liquids, creating an S-wave shadow zone — evidence of a liquid outer core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geometric correction:
options: Remove geometric distortions. Map projection. GCPs. RMSE < 1 pixel. Orthorectification
questions.solution: Geometric: model sensor + terrain. GCP: ground control points. Resampling: nearest, bilinear, cubic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mohr circle for stress: center = (σ₁+σ₃)/2, radius = (σ₁-σ₃)/2. For σ₁=100MPa, σ₃=40MPa:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Landslide susceptibility mapping:
options: Weighted overlay: slope, geology, rainfall, land use, distance to faults
questions.solution: Susceptibility: heuristic, statistical (logistic regression), deterministic (infinite slope). Machine learning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pressuremeter test:
options: In-situ stress-strain behavior. Modulus, limit pressure, creep pressure
questions.solution: Pressuremeter: expandable probe in borehole. Ménard pressuremeter. P_0, P_f, P_l.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Weathering types:
options: Physical (freeze-thaw, exfoliation, salt crystal growth) and chemical (dissolution, oxidation, hydrolysis)
questions.solution: Physical: mechanical breakup. Chemical: mineral alteration. Spheroidal weathering of jointed rock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 10 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Von Mises stress criterion:
questions.solution: Von Mises: equivalent stress σ_eq = √((σ₁-σ₂)²+(σ₂-σ₃)²+(σ₃-σ₁)²)/√2.
